column4: Validate that the POST /api/v1/briefs endpoint accepts a well-formed JSON brief and returns a job ID
column5: API Gateway and Brief Submission Lambda deployed; valid Cognito JWT with artifacts:generate scope available
column6:
1. Authenticate as a pre-sales consultant and obtain a Cognito access token
2. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with a valid JSON brief payload
3. Verify HTTP 202 response
4. Verify response body contains solution_id UUID
5. Verify DynamoDB solution-state record created with status QUEUED
column7: HTTP 202 returned; solution_id UUID present in response; DynamoDB record status = QUEUED within 5 seconds
column14: Primary happy-path for all pre-sales consultant usage

column4: Validate that the POST /api/v1/briefs endpoint rejects requests with missing or invalid Cognito tokens
column5: API Gateway and Cognito authoriser deployed
column6:
1. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with no Authorization header
2. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with an expired JWT
3. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with a tampered JWT signature
4. Verify all three attempts return HTTP 401
column7: HTTP 401 returned for all three unauthorized attempts; no DynamoDB record created
column14: Covers SOC 2 CC6.1 logical access control

column4: Validate that a delivery consultant cannot submit a brief (requires artifacts:generate scope)
column5: Cognito User Pool with all three groups deployed; delivery consultant test user available
column6:
1. Authenticate as a delivery-consultant user and obtain a Cognito access token
2. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with a valid brief payload
3. Verify HTTP 403 response
4. Verify no DynamoDB record was created
column7: HTTP 403 Forbidden returned; no SQS message enqueued
column14: Validates role-based scope enforcement per Detailed Design Identity and Access Management section

column4: Validate that the Brief Submission Lambda rejects payloads with missing required fields and returns descriptive errors
column5: API Gateway deployed; valid Cognito JWT available
column6:
1. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with an empty JSON object {}
2. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with client_name omitted
3. Send POST /api/v1/briefs with artifact_types as an empty array
4. Verify all three attempts return HTTP 400 with field-level error messages
column7: HTTP 400 returned for all invalid payloads; response body contains descriptive error identifying the missing field; no SQS message enqueued
column14: Test all documented required fields per API schema

column3: SQS Enqueue and Step Functions Trigger
column4: Validate that a submitted brief results in an SQS FIFO message being enqueued and a Step Functions workflow being started
column5: Brief Submission Lambda and SQS FIFO queue deployed; EventBridge Pipes connecting SQS to Step Functions configured
column6:
1. Submit a valid brief via POST /api/v1/briefs
2. Verify an SQS FIFO message appears in amatra-generation-queue-{env}.fifo within 5 seconds
3. Verify a Step Functions standard workflow execution is started for the solution_id
4. Verify DynamoDB solution record status transitions from QUEUED to PROCESSING
column7: SQS message enqueued with correct solution_id; Step Functions execution started; DynamoDB status = PROCESSING within 30 seconds
column14: Validates async pipeline trigger; covers SQS FIFO and EventBridge Pipes integration

column4: Validate that the Bedrock Orchestration Lambda retrieves the prompt template from S3 and invokes Bedrock InvokeModel for a single artifact type
column5: Bedrock Orchestration Lambda deployed; prompt templates uploaded to S3 prompts/ prefix; Secrets Manager Bedrock config secret populated
column6:
1. Trigger the Bedrock Orchestration Lambda via Step Functions with artifact type discovery-questionnaire
2. Verify the Lambda retrieves the prompt template from S3
3. Verify the Lambda calls Bedrock InvokeModel with Claude 3 Sonnet model ID
4. Verify the Lambda returns structured markdown output
column7: Bedrock InvokeModel called successfully; markdown output returned without error; CloudWatch logs show token counts; no ThrottlingException
column14: Use Dev environment to control Bedrock token consumption during testing

column3: Output Validation Lambda — Pass and Fail Paths
column4: Validate that the Output Validation Lambda accepts structurally complete artifacts and rejects incomplete ones
column5: Output Validation Lambda deployed; S3 artifacts bucket accessible
column6:
1. Invoke the Output Validation Lambda with a complete discovery-questionnaire artifact (all required sections present)
2. Verify the artifact is stored at {solution_id}/raw/ in S3
3. Invoke with an incomplete artifact (required sections missing)
4. Verify the artifact is stored at {solution_id}/failed/ and DynamoDB status updated to VALIDATION_FAILED
column7: Complete artifact stored at raw/ S3 prefix; incomplete artifact stored at failed/ prefix; DynamoDB status accurately reflects VALIDATION_FAILED
column14: Tests the Processing Integrity SOC 2 control PI1.1

column4: Validate that GET /api/v1/briefs/{solution_id}/status returns accurate job status at each pipeline stage
column5: Full async pipeline deployed; jobs in QUEUED / PROCESSING / COMPLETE states available in Dev
column6:
1. Poll GET /api/v1/briefs/{solution_id}/status while job is QUEUED
2. Poll again while job is PROCESSING
3. Poll again after job is COMPLETE
4. Verify each response reflects the current DynamoDB status
5. Verify COMPLETE response includes artifact_manifest
column7: Each poll returns the correct status enum; COMPLETE response includes artifact_manifest with S3 keys for all artifact types
column14: Critical for the consultant monitoring user experience

column4: Validate that GET /api/v1/briefs/{solution_id}/artifacts returns valid pre-signed S3 download URLs for all generated artifacts
column5: Artifact Retrieval Lambda deployed; a COMPLETE solution with all 7 artifact types stored in S3 available
column6:
1. Call GET /api/v1/briefs/{solution_id}/artifacts with a valid presales-consultant JWT
2. Verify the response contains pre-signed URLs for all 7 artifact types
3. Download each artifact via the pre-signed URL
4. Verify each download succeeds with HTTP 200
5. Wait 1 hour and retry a URL — verify HTTP 403
column7: All 7 artifact pre-signed URLs returned; all download successfully within 1-hour window; expired URL returns HTTP 403 after 1 hour
column14: Validates 1-hour URL expiry security control per Detailed Design Data Protection section

column4: Validate the complete generation pipeline for all 7 artifact types from a simple client brief submission through to artifact download
column5: Full platform deployed in Staging; representative simple client brief provided by Head of Solutions; valid pre-sales consultant test account available
column6:
1. Log in as a pre-sales consultant and obtain a Cognito JWT
2. Submit a simple client brief requesting all 7 artifact types
3. Monitor job status every 2 minutes
4. Verify job reaches COMPLETE status within 90 minutes
5. Download all 7 generated artifacts
6. Score each artifact against the internal QA rubric
7. Verify SES job completion email received
column7: All 7 artifacts generated and downloadable; Step Functions completes in ≤90 minutes; no DLQ messages; SES completion email received; artifacts pass QA rubric on first review
column14: Primary Phase 4 test; target ≥90% first-review QA pass rate per SOW Success Metrics

column4: Validate the complete pipeline with a complex multi-requirement brief to confirm AI output quality scales with brief complexity
column5: Full platform deployed in Staging; complex representative brief (financial services with SOC 2 requirements) provided by Head of Solutions
column6:
1. Submit a complex brief with compliance requirements (SOC 2 Type II) requesting all 7 artifact types
2. Monitor job status until COMPLETE
3. Download and QA-score each artifact against the internal rubric
4. Verify execution duration ≤90 minutes under normal load
column7: All 7 artifacts generated; QA rubric pass on first review; complex compliance requirements reflected accurately in SOW and detailed design; execution ≤90 minutes
column14: Second of three representative brief complexities required by SOW Testing section

column4: Validate that the platform enforces per-user monthly generation limits and blocks submissions beyond the configured threshold
column5: Admin Governance Lambda deployed; a test user with per-user limit set to 2 engagements/month configured in DynamoDB
column6:
1. Submit 2 valid briefs as the rate-limited test user and verify both succeed (HTTP 202)
2. Attempt to submit a 3rd brief as the same user
3. Verify HTTP 429 is returned
4. Call GET /api/v1/admin/usage and verify usage counter shows 2/2
5. Set the per-user limit to 5 via PUT /api/v1/admin/usage/limits/user/{user_id}
6. Verify the 3rd brief submission now succeeds
column7: HTTP 429 returned on 3rd submission; admin can override limit; updated limit allows further submissions; all limit changes logged in DynamoDB audit table with admin user ID and timestamp
column14: Validates cost-control and governance commitments from SOW Governance section

column4: Validate all admin console operations for per-user and global monthly usage limits
column5: Admin Governance Lambda deployed; Cognito admin test user with platform:admin scope available
column6:
1. Call GET /api/v1/admin/usage and verify stats are returned
2. Call PUT /api/v1/admin/usage/limits/global with a new limit value
3. Verify the global limit is updated in DynamoDB
4. Call PUT /api/v1/admin/usage/limits/user/{user_id} for a specific user
5. Verify DynamoDB audit record contains admin user ID and timestamp
6. Attempt all admin endpoints with a presales-consultant JWT and verify HTTP 403
column7: All CRUD operations succeed for admin users; HTTP 403 returned for non-admin access; all limit changes produce DynamoDB audit records with correct metadata
column14: SOC 2 evidence — admin action auditability

column4: Validate that a Bedrock permanent failure routes the job to the DLQ and triggers a CloudWatch alarm
column5: Full async pipeline deployed; CloudWatch DLQ alarm configured; test Lambda override configured to simulate ModelNotReadyException
column6:
1. Submit a brief configured to trigger a permanent Bedrock error
2. Verify Step Functions error handler transitions to HandlePermanentFailure state
3. Verify the SQS message is moved to amatra-generation-dlq-{env} after 3 attempts
4. Verify DynamoDB solution record updated to FAILED with error_detail populated
5. Verify DLQ-Message-Received CloudWatch alarm fires within 5 minutes
6. Verify SNS notification email sent to operations team
column7: Job appears in DLQ; DynamoDB status = FAILED; CloudWatch alarm transitions to ALARM within 5 minutes; SNS notification received by operations team
column14: Critical for SOC 2 Processing Integrity — ensures no silent failures

column3: SES Completion Notification — Success and Failure
column4: Validate that job completion emails are sent for both COMPLETE and FAILED terminal states
column5: SES Notification Lambda deployed; verified sender email in SES; Step Functions terminal states wired to SES Lambda
column6:
1. Complete a successful end-to-end generation job
2. Verify SES sends a completion email within 5 minutes of COMPLETE status
3. Trigger a generation failure (permanent Bedrock error)
4. Verify SES sends a failure notification email to the submitting consultant
column7: Completion email received within 5 minutes of COMPLETE status; failure email received within 5 minutes of FAILED status
column14: Non-blocking — job completion not gated on email delivery per integration design

column4: Validate that users can refresh their Cognito access token and continue submitting briefs without re-authenticating
column5: Cognito User Pool deployed; test user with a nearly expired access token (short expiry set in Dev)
column6:
1. Obtain an access token with a short expiry
2. Allow the access token to expire
3. Use the Cognito refresh token endpoint to obtain a new access token
4. Submit a brief using the new access token
5. Verify HTTP 202 and successful job creation
column7: New access token issued successfully via refresh endpoint; subsequent brief submission succeeds; refresh token remains valid for 30-day window
column14: Validates 30-day refresh token design per Detailed Design Authentication and SSO Flows section

column3: Concurrent Job Load Test — 10 Simultaneous Briefs
column4: Validate that the async generation pipeline handles peak load of 10 concurrent brief submissions without DLQ messages and all jobs completing within 90 minutes
column5: Full Staging platform deployed; 10 test pre-sales consultant accounts provisioned; CloudWatch monitoring active
column6:
1. Simultaneously submit 10 valid briefs from 10 separate consultant accounts at T+0
2. Monitor SQS queue depth and Step Functions execution count
3. Poll all 10 solution IDs for status every 5 minutes
4. Record the time each job reaches COMPLETE status
5. Verify DLQ remains empty throughout the test
6. Verify API Gateway returns HTTP 202 for all 10 submissions within 5 seconds
column7: All 10 jobs reach COMPLETE status within 90 minutes; DLQ message count = 0 throughout; no 5xx errors; Step Functions success rate = 100%
column14: Primary async performance test per SOW Testing section — simulates peak load at 24 engagements per quarter

column4: Identify the platform throughput ceiling by submitting 25 concurrent briefs and measuring degradation behaviour
column5: Full Staging platform deployed; 25 test accounts provisioned; CloudWatch DLQ and Step Functions metrics active
column6:
1. Simultaneously submit 25 valid briefs from 25 separate accounts at T+0
2. Monitor SQS queue depth; Step Functions concurrent executions; Bedrock throttle events
3. Record completion times for all 25 jobs
4. Monitor for any DLQ messages and Lambda error rates
5. Document the observed throughput ceiling and graceful degradation behaviour
column7: All jobs eventually complete or fail with observable graceful degradation; Bedrock ThrottlingException retries captured; no data corruption or silent failures
column14: Establishes platform throughput ceiling per SOW Testing section — informs Bedrock quota increase request

column3: API Response Time — Job Submission P95
column4: Validate that POST /api/v1/briefs responds within 2 seconds at P95 under concurrent load
column5: Staging platform deployed; load test tool configured for 50 concurrent users
column6:
1. Configure load test: 50 virtual users submitting POST /api/v1/briefs repeatedly over 5 minutes
2. Measure API Gateway P95 latency from CloudWatch
3. Confirm no Lambda cold-starts during the test window
4. Record P95 and P99 latency values
column7: API Gateway P95 latency < 2 seconds; P99 < 5 seconds; error rate < 1%; API-Latency-P95-Breach alarm does not fire
column14: SLA commitment per Detailed Design Workload Criticality section — triggers Provisioned Concurrency review if failed

column3: Endurance Test — Platform Stability Over 24 Hours
column4: Validate platform stability and confirm no memory leaks or performance degradation under sustained low-level load over 24 hours
column5: Staging platform deployed with CloudWatch monitoring; load test configured for 2 briefs/hour
column6:
1. Configure steady-state load: 2 brief submissions per hour for 24 hours
2. Monitor Lambda memory utilisation and execution duration trends
3. Monitor DynamoDB consumed capacity and throttle events
4. Monitor SQS queue depth for accumulation
5. Check CloudWatch error alarms at 4-hour intervals
6. Review X-Ray service map for latency anomalies at test end
column7: Lambda memory and duration metrics remain stable; no DLQ messages; DynamoDB throttle events = 0; all 48 jobs across 24 hours reach COMPLETE; no CloudWatch alarms fire
column14: Validates 99.9% availability design — run prior to Production go-live

column4: Validate that the API Gateway endpoint is resistant to OWASP API Security Top 10 attack categories
column5: OWASP ZAP configured; Staging API Gateway endpoint accessible; WAF v2 with OWASP Managed Rules active
column6:
1. Run OWASP ZAP automated API scan against Staging API Gateway with OpenAPI spec provided
2. Test API1 (BOLA): attempt to retrieve another user's solution_id via GET /api/v1/briefs/{other_solution_id}
3. Test API3 (Excessive Data Exposure): verify no internal stack traces in responses
4. Test API8 (Injection): submit SQL injection payloads in brief JSON fields
5. Review ZAP scan report for HIGH and CRITICAL findings
column7: OWASP ZAP scan reports zero HIGH or CRITICAL findings; BOLA attempt returns HTTP 403; no internal metadata exposed; injection payloads blocked by WAF with HTTP 403
column14: Required for go-live quality gate; results included in SOC 2 evidence package

column3: AWS WAF Rule Effectiveness — Rate Limiting and Injection
column4: Validate that WAF v2 enforces rate limiting at 1000 req/min/IP and blocks SQL injection and XSS payloads
column5: WAF v2 Web ACL attached to Staging API Gateway with rate limit rule and OWASP CommonRuleSet active
column6:
1. Send SQL injection payload in POST /api/v1/briefs client_name field
2. Send XSS payload in GET /api/v1/briefs query parameter
3. Send 1100 GET /api/v1/health requests within 60 seconds from a single IP
4. Verify WAF CloudWatch metrics show blocked requests for steps 1–3
5. Verify legitimate requests after the rate limit window resume successfully
column7: All injection attempts return HTTP 403; rate-limit breach requests return HTTP 429; WAF BlockedRequests CloudWatch metric increments; legitimate requests resume after 60-second window
column14: Validates WAF design per Detailed Design Network Security section; results included in SOC 2 evidence package

column3: Encryption Verification — At Rest and In Transit
column4: Validate that all data stores use SSE-KMS encryption and all data in transit enforces TLS 1.2 or higher
column5: All Staging AWS infrastructure deployed via Terraform
column6:
1. Run aws s3api get-bucket-encryption for amatra-artifacts-{env} and verify SSEAlgorithm = aws:kms
2. Run aws dynamodb describe-table for both DynamoDB tables and verify SSEDescription.Status = ENABLED
3. Verify CloudTrail S3 bucket uses amatra-audit CMK
4. Test TLS version by connecting to API Gateway with TLS 1.1 and verify connection rejected
5. Verify Secrets Manager secrets show KMS key ID in describe-secret output
column7: All S3 buckets encrypted with SSE-KMS; both DynamoDB tables KMS-encrypted; TLS 1.1 connections rejected; all Secrets Manager secrets encrypted with amatra-secrets CMK
column14: Required SOC 2 CC6.7 evidence; KMS key ARNs documented in SOC 2 evidence package

column3: Cognito Authentication Bypass and Token Manipulation
column4: Validate that the API cannot be accessed by manipulating or bypassing Cognito JWT validation
column5: API Gateway Cognito authoriser deployed; security testing tools available for JWT manipulation
column6:
1. Attempt API access with no Authorization header — expect HTTP 401
2. Attempt with a valid-structure JWT signed with a wrong private key — expect HTTP 401
3. Attempt with a valid JWT where exp claim is in the past — expect HTTP 401
4. Attempt with a valid JWT where scope is manually elevated from artifacts:read to artifacts:generate — expect HTTP 403
5. Attempt session replay of a previously revoked token
column7: All unauthorized and tampered token attempts return correct HTTP 4xx errors; no backend Lambda invocations recorded for rejected requests; CloudWatch Cognito auth failure metrics increment
column14: Validates JWT validation design per Detailed Design Authentication and SSO Flows section — SOC 2 CC6.1

column4: Validate that all required API operations are captured in CloudTrail and the audit log is immutable
column5: CloudTrail multi-region trail active; CloudTrail S3 bucket with Object Lock enabled; Staging platform operational for at least 24 hours
column6:
1. Perform auditable actions: submit a brief; retrieve artifacts; update a usage limit as admin; change a Cognito user group membership
2. Query CloudTrail event history for each of the 4 action types
3. Verify all 4 events appear in CloudTrail within 15 minutes of the action
4. Verify CloudTrail log files in S3 have Object Lock WORM metadata applied
5. Attempt to delete a CloudTrail log file from S3 and verify the delete is denied
column7: All 4 action types captured in CloudTrail; Object Lock prevents deletion; Cognito group change logged; usage limit change logged in both DynamoDB audit table and CloudTrail
column14: Core SOC 2 evidence artefact — completeness must be verified before go-live

column4: Validate that the CloudWatch Synthetics canary correctly measures API availability and the Platform-Availability-Critical alarm fires on sustained 5xx errors
column5: CloudWatch Synthetics canary deployed and running every 1 minute against /api/v1/health; SNS alarm routing validated
column6:
1. Confirm canary has been running for 24 hours with PASSED status in Staging
2. Introduce controlled failure by setting Brief Submission Lambda reserved concurrency to 0
3. Monitor API Gateway 5xx rate in CloudWatch
4. Verify Platform-Availability-Critical alarm transitions to ALARM within 5 minutes of 5xx rate exceeding 1%
5. Verify SNS notification email received by operations team
6. Restore Lambda concurrency and verify alarm returns to OK state
column7: Canary reports PASSED under normal operation; alarm fires within 5 minutes; SNS notification received within 2 minutes; alarm self-recovers when errors resolve
column14: Validates 99.9% SLA monitoring — alarm thresholds may be tuned during hypercare

column4: Validate that the DynamoDB solution-state table can be restored to a point-in-time within the previous hour within the RPO of 1 hour
column5: DynamoDB PITR enabled on amatra-solution-state-{env}; known set of solution records inserted at T-30min; DR runbook SOP-OPS-003 available
column6:
1. Record the number of solution records in the table at T-0
2. Delete 3 test solution records to simulate accidental data loss
3. Initiate DynamoDB PITR restoration to T-30min using the AWS CLI
4. Measure wall-clock time from restoration initiation to completion
5. Query the restored table and verify the 3 deleted records are present
6. Verify restoration completes within 1 hour
column7: PITR restoration completes within 1 hour; all 3 deleted records present in restored table; restored table is queryable immediately; restoration time documented for SOC 2 evidence
column14: Validates RPO = 1 hour commitment; result included in SOC 2 evidence package per SOW Testing section

column3: Disaster Recovery — Lambda Redeployment from IaC
column4: Validate that all Lambda functions can be redeployed from Terraform IaC within the RTO of 4 hours following a simulated catastrophic failure
column5: Terraform modules for compute layer available in GitHub; ECR container images available; Staging environment operational
column6:
1. Record current Lambda function versions and aliases for all 9 functions
2. Simulate failure by deleting all 9 Lambda functions from the Staging environment
3. Trigger a full Terraform apply of the compute module from the GitHub Actions pipeline
4. Measure wall-clock time from pipeline trigger to all 9 Lambda health checks passing
5. Verify all Lambda functions restored with correct reserved concurrency
6. Run the end-to-end health check script
column7: All 9 Lambda functions redeployed; wall-clock time ≤ 2 hours for compute layer redeployment; health check passes for all functions
column14: Validates RTO = 4 hours; Terraform-only recovery approach; result included in SOC 2 evidence package

column4: Validate that all SOC 2 Type II control evidence artefacts are present and cover the five Trust Service Criteria
column5: All platform security components deployed; CloudTrail active for ≥30 days; AWS Config evaluating all baseline rules
column6:
1. Export CloudTrail log files for the past 30 days and verify they cover all required event types
2. Run aws configservice get-compliance-details-by-config-rule for each of the 5 SOC 2 baseline Config rules and verify all are COMPLIANT
3. Export Secrets Manager rotation logs and verify all secrets show last_rotated_date within 30 days
4. Export IAM Access Analyzer findings and verify no unintended resource sharing exists
5. Verify KMS key rotation status for all 4 CMKs shows rotation_enabled = True
column7: All CloudTrail event types present with no gaps; all 5 Config rules COMPLIANT; all secrets rotated within 30 days; IAM Access Analyzer shows zero active findings; all 4 KMS keys have rotation enabled
column14: Must be completed by Week 25 per SOW deliverable schedule; artefacts included in SOC 2 Evidence Package (Deliverable 20)

column4: Validate that all customer data is stored exclusively in us-west-2 and no cross-region transfer occurs for primary data storage
column5: All S3 buckets and DynamoDB tables deployed via Terraform in us-west-2; S3 cross-region replication configured for DR only
column6:
1. Verify S3 bucket amatra-artifacts-prod region is us-west-2 using aws s3api get-bucket-location
2. Verify DynamoDB tables are in us-west-2 using aws dynamodb describe-table
3. Submit a brief and verify the generated artifact S3 object has DataResidency=us-west-2 metadata tag
4. Verify the S3 DR replication rule replicates to us-east-1 DR bucket only
5. Verify S3 object metadata includes Classification=Confidential tag
column7: All primary data stored in us-west-2; artifact S3 objects tagged with DataResidency=us-west-2 and Classification=Confidential; DR replication bucket in us-east-1 is read-only
column14: GDPR Article 25 compliance requirement; data residency certificate included in SOC 2 evidence package

column4: Validate that Cognito enforces MFA for all users in the admins group and non-MFA logins are rejected
column5: Cognito User Pool advanced security mode enabled; admin test user enrolled in TOTP MFA; a second admin test user with no MFA device registered
column6:
1. Attempt to authenticate as the non-MFA admin user and obtain a Cognito access token without completing MFA
2. Verify the authentication flow requires MFA challenge before issuing access token
3. Complete MFA successfully for the TOTP-enrolled admin user
4. Verify the resulting JWT contains the platform:admin scope only after MFA completion
5. Verify presales-consultant users can authenticate without MFA
column7: Non-MFA admin login blocked at MFA challenge — access token not issued without MFA; TOTP-enrolled admin receives platform:admin scope after MFA; presales-consultant users authenticate without MFA
column14: SOC 2 CC6.1 requirement — mandatory for go-live quality gate

column3: Pre-Sales Consultant — Submit Brief and Download Artifacts
column4: Validate that a real pre-sales consultant can submit a client brief and receive all 7 generated artifacts within the production-equivalent environment without technical assistance
column5: UAT Staging environment deployed; 5–10 pre-sales consultants provisioned with Cognito UAT accounts; TRN-006 completed by all UAT participants
column6:
1. Each UAT participant logs in using their Cognito credentials at the Staging platform URL
2. Each participant submits a representative client brief using their own realistic client scenario
3. Participants monitor job status over the 30–90 minute generation window
4. Each participant downloads all 7 generated artifacts on job completion
5. Each participant scores each artifact using the internal QA rubric (completeness, accuracy, formatting, usability)
6. Participants complete the structured UAT feedback form
column7: ≥90% of submitted briefs receive a first-review QA pass across all 7 artifact types; all participants complete the workflow without technical assistance; SES completion email received within 5 minutes
column14: Primary UAT scenario — ≥90% first-review pass rate required for Go-Live Sign-Off per SOW User Acceptance Testing section

column3: Artifact Quality — SOW and Solution Briefing Content Accuracy
column4: Validate that Bedrock-generated SOW and Solution Briefing accurately reflect the client brief details and meet consulting-grade content standards
column5: UAT Staging environment; representative client brief with defined AWS scope and compliance requirement submitted by Head of Solutions
column6:
1. Head of Solutions submits a pre-prepared representative brief with defined AWS scope and compliance requirement
2. Upon COMPLETE status, download the generated SOW and Solution Briefing artifacts
3. Review SOW scope section: verify in-scope services match the brief; verify out-of-scope items are reasonable
4. Review Solution Briefing: verify business opportunity slide reflects the stated client pain points
5. Review investment summary section in SOW for plausibility
6. Score both artifacts on the internal QA rubric
column7: SOW scope section accurately reflects the technology stack from the brief; Solution Briefing business opportunity slide correctly frames client pain points; both artifacts score ≥80% on the QA rubric
column14: Critical for validating Bedrock prompt quality — iterative prompt tuning triggered if QA score < 80%

column3: Artifact Quality — Detailed Design and Infrastructure Cost Model
column4: Validate that the generated Detailed Design and Infrastructure Cost Model are technically accurate and suitable for delivery to clients
column5: UAT Staging environment; a technology-specific brief (AWS serverless with Lambda and DynamoDB) submitted
column6:
1. Submit a brief specifying a serverless AWS architecture scenario
2. Download the generated Detailed Design artifact
3. Review the architecture components section for technical accuracy
4. Review the generated Infrastructure Cost Model CSV for correct service names and plausibility of cost estimates
5. Score both artifacts using the internal QA rubric
6. Note any factual errors for prompt tuning feedback
column7: Detailed Design architecture section references correct AWS services relevant to the brief; Infrastructure Cost Model contains plausible cost estimates; both artifacts score ≥80% on QA rubric; no critical factual errors
column14: Delivery-critical artifacts — ML/AI Engineer reviews prompt tuning feedback from this test

column4: Validate that a pre-sales consultant can update a brief and submit a regeneration request that produces an improved artifact set
column5: UAT Staging environment; a previously generated artifact set with known quality gaps identified by Head of Solutions; TRN-008 completed by UAT participant
column6:
1. Identify a previously generated SOW with a specific content gap (e.g. missing compliance section)
2. Update the original brief JSON to add the missing compliance requirement detail
3. Re-submit the brief via POST /api/v1/briefs (new solution_id generated)
4. Download the regenerated SOW artifact
5. Verify the compliance section is now present and accurately reflects the updated brief
6. Compare QA rubric score against the original generation
column7: Regenerated SOW includes the previously missing compliance section; QA rubric score improves; workflow is intuitive without technical assistance; completes within 90 minutes
column14: Validates regeneration workflow trained in TRN-008 — critical for ≥90% first-pass rate improvement loop

column3: Platform Usability — Brief Submission Without Technical Assistance
column4: Validate that a pre-sales consultant who has completed TRN-006 can submit a brief and retrieve artifacts independently using only the user guide
column5: UAT Staging environment; UAT participant who has completed TRN-006 but has not been shown the workflow in a demo; Pre-Sales Consultant User Guide provided
column6:
1. Provide the UAT participant with only the platform URL, their Cognito credentials, and the user guide
2. Ask the participant to submit a brief of their choice requesting all 7 artifact types without facilitator assistance
3. Ask the participant to monitor job status and download artifacts upon completion
4. Record any points where the participant requires assistance
5. Collect usability feedback via the structured UAT feedback form
column7: UAT participant completes the full workflow (login, submit, monitor, download) without facilitator assistance; zero blocking issues requiring assistance; participant rates usability ≥4 out of 5
column14: Usability gate for go-live — recurring blockers fed back to TRN-006 content and user guide

column3: Delivery Consultant — Retrieve Assigned Delivery Artifacts
column4: Validate that a delivery consultant can retrieve generated delivery artifacts but cannot submit new briefs
column5: UAT Staging environment; a COMPLETE solution with all 7 artifact types available; delivery consultant UAT account with artifacts:read scope only provisioned
column6:
1. Authenticate as a delivery consultant and obtain a Cognito JWT
2. Attempt POST /api/v1/briefs — verify HTTP 403 is returned
3. Call GET /api/v1/briefs/{solution_id}/artifacts for an assigned solution
4. Download the detailed-design.md and implementation-guide.md artifacts
5. Verify the delivery consultant cannot access solutions belonging to other users
column7: HTTP 403 on brief submission; delivery artifacts downloaded successfully; consultant cannot access other users' solutions; Delivery Team UAT participants rate the artifact content as fit for downstream delivery use
column14: Validates Phase 2 delivery team access model per SOW User Acceptance Testing section

column4: Validate that all migrated Okta users can successfully log in to the Staging platform using their new Cognito credentials without access disruption
column5: Okta-to-Cognito migration completed in Staging; all ~120 users have received temporary Cognito credentials; Staging platform operational
column6:
1. Coordinate with a sample cohort of 10–20 Okta-migrated users covering all three Cognito groups
2. Ask each user to log in at the Staging Cognito Hosted UI using their temporary password
3. Ask each user to complete the mandatory password reset on first login
4. Verify each user lands in the correct Cognito group
5. Record success and failure counts; investigate any failures
6. Calculate login success rate
column7: ≥95% of sampled migrated users log in successfully on first attempt; all users complete the password reset; group assignments match the original Okta group mapping; login success rate documented for go-live quality gate
column14: Go-live quality gate criterion per SOW Go-Live Readiness — ≥95% success rate required

column4: Validate that all go-live readiness criteria are fully satisfied and obtain formal written approval from Head of Solutions and CTO before Production deployment
column5: All Phase 4 test phases completed; SOC 2 evidence package assembled; all Severity 1 and 2 defects resolved; CloudWatch dashboards validated in Staging; operational runbooks reviewed by VP Engineering
column6:
1. QA Engineer presents the completed test results report covering all functional, integration, performance, security, and UAT results
2. Security Lead reviews and signs off on the SOC 2 evidence package
3. Head of Solutions formally signs the UAT Sign-Off document confirming ≥90% artifact first-review pass rate
4. CTO and VP Engineering review the Go-Live Readiness Checklist (all 10 items must be checked)
5. CTO provides formal written go-live approval
6. Rollback procedure is confirmed as tested and documented
column7: All go-live readiness checklist items checked; UAT Sign-Off signed by Head of Solutions; formal written go-live approval from CTO and VP Engineering obtained; all Severity 1 and 2 defects confirmed resolved
column14: This test produces SOW Deliverable 22 (Go-Live Readiness Sign-Off); must be completed by Week 26 (Milestone M4)

column3: Admin Usage Governance — Business Scenario Validation
column4: Validate that the Head of Solutions and designated admins can perform day-to-day usage governance tasks without technical assistance
column5: UAT Staging environment; admin UAT participant who has completed TRN-002 and TRN-003; usage data populated from earlier UAT brief submissions
column6:
1. Admin UAT participant logs in with Cognito admin credentials (MFA enrolled)
2. Admin calls GET /api/v1/admin/usage and reviews per-user submission counts for the UAT cohort
3. Admin identifies a UAT user approaching their limit and adjusts their per-user limit via PUT /api/v1/admin/usage/limits/user/{user_id}
4. Admin verifies the DynamoDB audit record for the limit change shows the admin user ID and timestamp
5. Admin calls GET /api/v1/admin/audit and verifies the last 5 admin actions are visible
6. Admin rates the usability of the governance workflow via the UAT feedback form
column7: Admin successfully completes all governance tasks without technical assistance; usage statistics are accurate; per-user limit change takes effect immediately; audit log shows correct admin action history; admin rates governance usability ≥4 out of 5
column14: Validates business-critical governance capability and TRN-002/TRN-003 training content effectiveness

column3: Platform Throughput — Business Value Metric Validation
column4: Validate that the platform demonstrably reduces artifact turnaround time and positions Amatra to achieve the ≥24 engagements per quarter throughput target
column5: Staging platform operational; at least 5 complete brief-to-artifact generation cycles documented during UAT; manual baseline turnaround time of 3 weeks established from Phase 1 discovery
column6:
1. Measure the average wall-clock time from brief submission to all 7 artifacts downloaded across the UAT cohort
2. Compare measured average turnaround time against the 3-week manual baseline
3. Calculate the percentage improvement in turnaround time
4. Based on observed job completion times and the 10-concurrent-job load test results, project achievable quarterly throughput
5. Present findings to Head of Solutions and CTO in the go-live readiness review
column7: Average AI-powered turnaround time is under 2 business days (demonstrating >90% reduction vs 3-week baseline); projected quarterly throughput at observed job completion rates supports ≥24 engagements per quarter target
column14: Business value validation confirming SOW Success Metrics are met — key input to post-go-live hypercare optimisation report
